architecture_fit.note: Single attention block, minimal delta from base_model.py; genuinely squares via the QK bilinear form and nails canonical degree-2, but only evaluates x^2 and fails on x^3.

baseline_comparison.note: Clean strawman: best-affine linear baseline (R^2~0) plus a linear-QK ablation, both far below the mechanism's R^2~0.999.

faithfulness.note: Linear-QK ablation removes the quadratic score and collapses R^2 to the baseline floor, isolating the QK square as the load-bearing component.

operating_range.note: Scale sweep 0.03->30 (>3 orders) holds R^2~0.999 with scale-adaptive beta and honestly shows fixed-beta degrading at the edges.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Fully hand-set circuit (beta, b0 fixed; affine readout calibrated from input distribution only, never targets) reproduces x^2.

visual_judgement.note: Three purposeful panels: parabola scatter for the claim, 3-bar ablation R^2 for causality, log-scale operating-range curve.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies each panel against the goal: right y-axis (R^2 on x^2), right baseline/ablation comparison, right operating-range grain.

notes: A clean, fully hand-built single-attention-block circuit that physically squares its input inside the QK bilinear score, hitting R^2~0.999 on the canonical degree-2 task versus a linear baseline at ~0. Faithfulness (linear-QK ablation collapses to floor), operating range (>3 orders with honest fixed-beta failure), and visualisation/rationale are all strong. The one real limitation is scope: it solves x^2 only and does not generalize to x^3, which keeps a goal titled 'polyeval' from being fully addressed.
